summary: PR 2664 links `ftkirbyyoshiegg.c` by changing its configure status from NonMatching to Matching. The main code change makes `ftKb_SpecialNYs_8010AC78` match by moving an `ftHurtboxInit` local into the caller, passing it into `inlineB0`, keeping a narrow `PAD_STACK(0x10)` in the caller, using `GET_JOBJ(victim)`, and updating `ftKb_SpecialNYs_801093A0` to accept a `Fighter_GObj*` argument at the declaration, definition, and call site. Symbol metadata also changes several ftKirby `.sdata` string and `.sdata2` float entries from global address-style names to local labels. Bot report evidence records one new match for `ftKb_SpecialNYs_8010AC78` and linked-code progress.

what_changed: Changed `ftkirbyyoshiegg.c` from NonMatching to Matching in `configure.py`.
Matched `ftKb_SpecialNYs_8010AC78` according to the bot report: before 99.32%, after 100.00%, +5 bytes for the unit item.
Changed `inlineB0` from allocating its own `ftHurtboxInit hurt` local to accepting `ftHurtboxInit* hurt` from its caller.
Moved `PAD_STACK(0x10)` and `ftHurtboxInit hurt` into `ftKb_SpecialNYs_8010AC78`, then called `inlineB0(victim, &hurt)`.
Replaced direct `victim->hsd_obj` access with `GET_JOBJ(victim)` for the `HSD_JObj* jobj` local.

Changed `ftKb_SpecialNYs_801093A0` prototype, definition, and call site to include `Fighter_GObj* gobj`, even though the shown function body still returns `ft_80459B88.hats[FTKIND_SAMUS]->hat_dynamics[0]`.
Changed five symbol entries from global `ftKb_Init_*` labels to local `@...` labels for `.sdata` strings and `.sdata2` floats.

smart_moves.text: Kept the signature recovery consistent across declaration, definition, and call site for `ftKb_SpecialNYs_801093A0`, avoiding a source-local prototype mismatch.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftkirbyspecialdonkey.h prototype update
raw_diff: ftkirbyspecialyoshi.c definition update
raw_diff: ftkirbyyoshiegg.c call changes to ftKb_SpecialNYs_801093A0(gobj)

smart_moves.text: Narrowed stack-shape steering to the matching function by moving `PAD_STACK(0x10)` into `ftKb_SpecialNYs_8010AC78` rather than leaving it in the inline helper.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftkirbyyoshiegg.c removes PAD_STACK(16) from inlineB0 and adds PAD_STACK(0x10) in ftKb_SpecialNYs_8010AC78

smart_moves.text: Used the existing `GET_JOBJ` accessor in the matched function instead of direct `victim->hsd_obj` access.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftkirbyyoshiegg.c changes HSD_JObj* jobj = victim->hsd_obj to HSD_JObj* jobj = GET_JOBJ(victim)

decomp_lessons.lesson: For small matching deltas involving inlined helpers, stack-local ownership can matter: moving a helper-local struct into the caller and passing a pointer may recover the expected stack layout.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: inlineB0 changes from local `ftHurtboxInit hurt` to pointer parameter
raw_diff: ftKb_SpecialNYs_8010AC78 adds caller-local `ftHurtboxInit hurt` and passes `&hurt`
text_corpus: bot report shows `ftKb_SpecialNYs_8010AC78` reached 100.00%
decomp_lessons.caveat: This is evidence for this PR's matched function only; do not generalize broad PAD_STACK or local-lifetime changes without objdiff/build evidence.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a function body or call shape requires a recovered parameter, update the owning header, definition, and call sites together.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftkirbyspecialdonkey.h, ftkirbyspecialyoshi.c, and ftkirbyyoshiegg.c all update `ftKb_SpecialNYs_801093A0` to take `Fighter_GObj* gobj`
decomp_lessons.caveat: The slice does not explain why the unused `gobj` parameter is required beyond the match result.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Move `ftHurtboxInit` allocation from inline helper to caller and pass it by pointer to influence stack layout while preserving typed field writes.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: inlineB0 signature and body use `ftHurtboxInit* hurt`
raw_diff: ftKb_SpecialNYs_8010AC78 declares `ftHurtboxInit hurt` and calls `inlineB0(victim, &hurt)`
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk: Stack/local-lifetime tactic; should remain evidence-backed and narrow.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use `PAD_STACK(0x10)` in the caller after adding the caller-local hurtbox struct.

text_corpus: bot report shows one new match for `ftKb_SpecialNYs_8010AC78`

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use accessor macro `GET_JOBJ(victim)` for `HSD_JObj* jobj` rather than direct `victim->hsd_obj` field access.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk: Loaded global standard says GET_JOBJ is not unconditional cleanup; here the PR evidence only supports it for this function.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Caller-owned struct can be a narrow stack-layout matching tactic for inlined helper code
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR 2664, `ftKb_SpecialNYs_8010AC78` reached 100% after `ftHurtboxInit hurt` moved from `inlineB0` into the caller, `inlineB0` accepted `ftHurtboxInit*`, and the caller passed `&hurt` with a narrow `PAD_STACK(0x10)`. Treat as a PR-local tactic, not a broad style rule.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftkirbyyoshiegg.c inlineB0 and ftKb_SpecialNYs_8010AC78 changes
text_corpus: bot report lists ftKb_SpecialNYs_8010AC78 after 100.00%
